original_hypothesis.title: LPRefund.manualRefund Lacks Txhash Uniqueness Check - Admin Can Issue Double Refunds
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: LPRefund.manualRefund uses `refunds[txhash] += amount` instead of checking if the txhash was already used. Unlike the regular refund() function which reverts on duplicate txhashes, manualRefund can be called multiple times with the same txhash, each time transferring additional taxToken to the recipient. This allows ADMIN_ROLE to drain the entire taxToken balance by repeatedly refunding the same transaction.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The asymmetry between refund() (which has replay protection) and manualRefund() (which doesn't) suggests a code oversight. The += operator accumulates amounts without any upper bound check, enabling fund extraction limited only by contract balance.

proof_of_signal.pattern: refunds[txhash] += amount; no check for existing refund; contrast with refund() which checks if(refunds[txhash] > 0) revert TxHashExists

preconditions: ADMIN_ROLE is compromised or acts maliciously

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: refunds[txhash] += amount with no upper bound or uniqueness check
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Call manualRefund repeatedly with same txhash and attacker-controlled recipient
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Drain entire taxToken balance from LPRefund contract

economic_questions: How much taxToken is typically held in the LPRefund contract?
Is ADMIN_ROLE protected by multi-sig or timelock?
Are there monitoring systems for manualRefund calls?
validation_requirements: Confirm manualRefund can be called with duplicate txhash
Verify no additional checks prevent repeated calls

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to entire LPRefund taxToken balance

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Legitimate refund recipients cannot be paid

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
